body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_14)
Regime data: VIX 17.18 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 63.6%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: TRADE | NEUTRAL conf 51.666666666666664% | Range 729.21–769.13 | iron_condor x1
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows elevated volatility with VIX at 17.18, a steep contango between 9-day (15.17) and 3-month (19.66) implied vols, and a cautious market breadth of 63.6%, while the 2-year and 10-year yields remain elevated at 4.21% and 4.56%, respectively, signaling persistent hawkish Fed expectations amid geopolitical tensions. SPY is under pressure at $749.17 with a negative sentiment of -2, reflecting the Nasdaq's sharp sell-off driven by escalating U.S.-Iran conflict, which is the key near-term catalyst to watch as it could trigger further risk-off moves. NVDA at $203.53 and AMZN at $247.31 both show high implied volatilities above 50% and positive sentiment, suggesting that despite the broader market weakness, these names are viewed as tactical longs—NVDA benefiting from its leadership in semis amid a generational tech shift, and AMZN presenting a rare value opportunity given it’s the cheapest in over a decade. The main risk is a potential inflation surprise this week that could prompt another Fed hike, as flagged by Fed’s Waller, which would likely exacerbate market volatility and pressure growth stocks further. Trade signal gates are fully active, indicating readiness for tactical positioning based on evolving geopolitical and macro data.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Nasdaq closes sharply lower as stocks fall

NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.sentiment_driver: Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade
AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Nasdaq closes sharply lower as stocks fall after latest U.S.-Iran escalation
Nasdaq leading stocks lower in final hour as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate — live
Inflation to fall for first time in 6 years. Consumers won't see much relief.
A Generational Transfer Is Happening: Amazon, Google, Microsoft Are the Losers. NVIDIA and Micron the Winners.
Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade. Has the Sell-Off Gone Too Far?
A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with major reports from BAC, JPM, WFC, GS on July 14 and MS on July 15. Investors will focus on credit quality trends and trading revenue as a barometer for economic resilience amid a VIX at 17.18 and HY spreads elevated at 269 bps. Financial sector results will influence risk sentiment broadly, impacting SPY given banks’ market weight, while subdued guidance could weigh on cyclical tech names like NVDA and consumer discretionary staples such as AMZN.
narratives.next_week: Next week features a tech and industrials tilt with GOOGL and TSLA earnings on July 22, followed by RTX, HON, and FCX reports. Market participants should watch for demand signals in tech and industrials as these sectors are bellwethers for growth and capital expenditure trends. Strong results could buoy SPY and reinforce bullish momentum in NVDA and AMZN, while any softness may pressure communication services and materials sectors, signaling caution on cyclical exposure.
narratives.next_month: The following month is packed with marquee tech earnings including META, MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, and AMD alongside the July 30 FOMC meeting and July 31 PCE inflation data. The Fed’s likely hold at 3.50% and inflation trajectory will be critical for equity multiples, particularly in growth-heavy names like NVDA and AMZN. Elevated inflation risks and mixed labor market signals from NFP and CPI releases in early August could increase volatility, prompting a defensive stance in SPY and selective positioning within technology and consumer discretionary sectors.
